name: BTC 1h Reset Then Go v1
edge_mechanism: a short-term directional move resets into fast structure, directional pressure reasserts, and continuation is entered with asymmetry coming from partial-first, runner-second exits
thesis: BTC 1h remains the better-fit continuation lane in the basket, but the prior continuation family was too sparse. This version drops ceremonial confirmation and uses a simpler reset-then-go sequence: pullback into structure, directional pressure recovery, then continuation with asymmetric exit logic.
invalidation_condition: If reset-then-go setups remain too sparse, or if continuation legs are too weak after the reset for the runner logic to matter, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A move pulls back into structure, pressure resets, then continuation resumes once directional participation improves again.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transitionary BTC 1h should still reward some continuation, but only after a reset that improves location. A simpler reset-and-go trigger should be denser than the prior continuation thesis while preserving some asymmetry.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is the best fit because it has more continuation potential than ETH 1h while remaining cleaner than AXS for a reset continuation idea.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: What looks like a reset may just be unresolved chop, producing many small starts with not enough true continuation legs.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first targets work but runners do not, keep the trigger and shift more size into earlier profits before touching entry logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Several modest first-target wins, some scratches, and a smaller set of runner outcomes that matter only when continuation is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 1h produces enough reset-then-go sequences to clear screen density and the exit structure shows better asymmetry than fixed targets alone.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trades, or a runner component that never contributes meaningful incremental value.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but denser than prior continuation families because the sequence is simplified to reset plus pressure recovery

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit lane for simplified reset continuation with asymmetric exits.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC 1h shows enough reset-then-go trades and the runner logic adds post-cost value.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h still behaves like unresolved chop and kills continuation too early.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with a mix of first-target wins and a few useful runners.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reset continuation logic survives on the cycle primary lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 1h preserves the same structure with smaller but cleaner moves.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h confirms continuation is the wrong mechanism for the tape.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, cleaner reset continuations than BTC.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same continuation grammar survives on a noisier lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS 1h preserves reset continuation with enough structure.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: AXS 1h turns the setup into noise.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Messier but sharper runner outcomes than BTC.

long: Stop loss: 0.86 * ATR_14 below entry or below EMA_55, whichever is tighter
short: Stop loss: 0.86 * ATR_14 above entry or above EMA_55, whichever is tighter
